title: Lemon Laced French Chicken Wings Confit
description: Savory, crisp chicken wings cooked in a butter and olive oil confit, finished in a cataplana and chilled in an ice-bath for ultimate texture.
instructions: Pat the chicken wings dry with paper towels and season all sides with salt and pepper.
In a large skillet, melt the butter and heat the olive oil over medium heat until the mixture foams.
Add the thyme, bay leaves, minced garlic, and lemon zest, and sauté for 2 minutes to infuse the aromatics.
Carefully place the seasoned wings into the skillet, turning occasionally, until the edges are golden and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Remove the wings from the skillet and let them cool to room temperature, then transfer them into a container.
Fill the container with ice cubes and pour cold lemon juice over the wings, creating an ice-bath shock that firms the meat and enhances crispness.
Seal the container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight, to allow flavors to develop.
Preheat a cataplana over medium-high heat and drizzle a small amount of olive oil.
Place the chilled wings into the cataplana and cook, turning once, until they are crusted and heated through, about 8 minutes.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional lemon zest and a drizzle of olive oil.
